overview: Bant Gearhulk leverages three-color flexibility to establish early board presence while ramping into high-value card advantage engines. By stabilizing with efficient removal and draw spells, the deck transitions into a late-game grind where massive creatures and recurring threats overwhelm opponents. The strategy prioritizes resource accumulation over early aggression, aiming to outlast and outvalue opposing decks in Standard.
win_conditions: Outvaluing opponents through Quantum Riddler's card draw and removal
Deploying large, resilient creatures that bypass early-game disruption
Generating excess mana with Nature's Rhythm to cast multiple threats per turn

keep_criteria: Hands with early mana sources and a one or two-drop
Draw spells or removal to stabilize against aggressive starts
Nature's Rhythm paired with a creature to accelerate curve
mulligan_criteria: Hands lacking early mana acceleration or interaction
Three or more high-cost cards without ramp
Hands missing a clear path to turn three or four

early_game: Play lands and low-cost creatures to establish board presence
Use early interaction to disrupt opponent's game plan
Prioritize drawing cards to find ramp and threats
mid_game: Cast Nature's Rhythm to accelerate mana production
Deploy Quantum Riddler to generate card advantage and remove blockers
Maintain board control while searching for win conditions
late_game: Cast large creatures that pressure the opponent's life total
Use excess mana to cast multiple spells per turn
Stabilize against late-game threats with removal and card draw

key_cards.name: Badgermole Cub
key_cards.role: Early-game board presence and token generation
key_cards.notes: Play on curve to establish early pressure and generate tokens that can block or trigger abilities. Use its low cost to stabilize against aggressive decks.

key_cards.name: Quantum Riddler
key_cards.notes: Prioritize casting when you have excess cards or need to clear a blocker. Use its draw ability to refill your hand and find answers, maintaining resource superiority.

key_cards.name: Nature's Rhythm
key_cards.notes: Cast on turn three or four to enable larger threats ahead of schedule. Ensure you have enough lands to support the extra mana, and use the generated mana to cast multiple spells or play a large creature.

sequencing_tips: Hold up removal for opponent's key threats before committing to your own plays
Cast Nature's Rhythm before playing large creatures to ensure you have enough mana
Use Quantum Riddler's draw ability to find answers before making a major board commitment
Prioritize playing lands and ramp before committing to high-cost spells

matchups.advice: Prioritize early interaction and board presence to slow down their early pressure. Use Badgermole Cub to generate blockers and stabilize the board.
Play around their removal by holding up counterspells or using Quantum Riddler to draw into answers. Ramp efficiently to outvalue them in the late game.
Race them by deploying threats quickly while maintaining card advantage. Use Nature's Rhythm to accelerate into larger creatures before they stabilize.
